kicker: ET LEVEL I // ROTATIONAL & FORWARD SPEED

beats.narration: This lab holds the part, the probe, the frequency, the filters. It changes only speed — and the calibrated signal changes anyway.

payload.title: CHANGE NOTHING BUT SPEED
payload.sub: Two speeds, two ways a good signal dies.
payload.panelLabel: ROTARY SCANNER // SPEED IS THE VARIABLE

beats.narration: A rotating coil drags its sensing zone across a fixed notch, so the signal is a pulse. Spin faster and that pulse narrows, climbing in frequency.
Your filters do not follow. A band-pass that frames the notch at fifteen hundred RPM starves it at six hundred and clips it at three thousand.

chips.s: Filters and speed are one pair

beats.narration: Now forward speed. Rotation plus feed traces a helix; pitch equals feed over rev rate. Open it past the coil and a short flaw survives by luck.

payload.heading: Open the pitch, leave a stripe
payload.note: PITCH = FEED / REV-RATE · PITCH > COIL = UNSWEPT STRIPES

beats.narration: To the electronics, speed is a calibration setting. Find both cliffs, document the window, and always verify at the speed you inspect.

payload.sub: Standardize dynamically — a static screen proves nothing.
